next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with comprehensive infectious workup including cytology for Strongyloides larvae and fungal stains
Check random serum cortisol level and consider stress-dose hydrocortisone for suspected adrenal insufficiency
Examine stool samples for Strongyloides larvae (O&P)
Perform abdominal CT with IV contrast to evaluate for bowel ischemia, perforation, or ischemic colitis
Check peripheral blood smear for organisms or schistocytes
Initiate empiric ivermectin if Strongyloides hyperinfection is suspected
Re-evaluate cat scratch site and perform biopsy/culture if any persistent skin lesions exist
